sub_sections.title: Project's pathways towards impact
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO delivers credible impact pathways against all five call expected impacts through its integrated research architecture and three TRL-5 sector demonstrators. CERIA's Decentralised Multi-Agent Coordination (WP4, D4-02) advances EO-02 by demonstrating coordination of ≥5 heterogeneous agents with ≥25% improvement in joint task completion over independent agent baselines and formal proof of protocol convergence. Against EI-01, NIHS's Healthcare Demonstrator — Clinical Decision Support (WP5) achieves diagnostic reasoning accuracy within 10% of specialist clinician performance on a benchmark of 500 de-identified clinical cases spanning 5 diagnostic domains (D5-02, M42); ISIA's Manufacturing Demonstrator — Process Optimisation (WP6) targets ≥15% defect reduction and ≥10% energy savings (D6-02, M42); and ELI's Logistics Transfer Demonstrator — Cross-Sector Generalisation (WP7) targets ≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time and ≥15% improvement in delivery schedule adherence, validated across 3 supply chain corridors (D7-02, M42). EI-02 is addressed through EDIH-mediated deployment targeting ≥3 hospital networks by M48, technology transfer to ≥5 manufacturing SMEs via ≥2 EDIHs, and ≥3 EDIHs actively disseminating MAESTRO; EI-03 through ≥20 publications in top AI venues (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AAMAS, AAAI) and AI-on-demand platform contributions (D8-02, D9-02), targeting adoption by ≥5 external research groups; EI-04 (inferred from destination placement) through CERIA's standardisation submission to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE by M48 (D4-01) and an open European-origin architecture reducing dependency on non-European platforms; and EI-05 through the open ecosystem strategy described in B.2.2. Target groups include European hospital networks and clinical informatics teams, manufacturing SMEs and factory operators via EIT Manufacturing, logistics operators and supply chain technology providers, AI researchers targeting top publication venues, and SMEs/startups seeking open European AI infrastructure. Key barriers include EU AI Act compliance timelines in healthcare (mitigated by NIHS-led validated dataset design and auditable reasoning traces), post-project adoption lags (mitigated by EDIH partnership agreements and ELI integration roadmap by M48), and competitive pressure from non-European AI platforms (mitigated by open-source release under Apache 2.0 and AI-on-demand platform contribution).

sub_sections.title: Measures to maximise impact - Dissemination, exploitation and communication
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO's dissemination plan targets four complementary audiences with sector-specific channels: (1) the AI research community receives ≥20 peer-reviewed publications in NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AAMAS, and AAAI (M12–M60) plus ≥3 invited keynotes, coordinated by ATU, BIIS, CERIA, and FIIT; (2) European hospital networks receive NIHS-led EDIH-facilitated engagement targeting ≥3 networks with post-project adoption planning by M48 (D5-02); (3) manufacturing SMEs receive ISIA-led technology transfer via ≥2 EDIHs and the EIT Manufacturing network targeting ≥5 SMEs by M48 (D6-02); and (4) the developer and SME community receives the BAL-led open-source MAESTRO framework (D9-02, M36) with ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months of release, AI-on-demand platform contribution, validation through ≥2 TEFs, and technology transfer through ≥3 EDIHs (OBJ-7). The exploitation strategy is sector-differentiated: NIHS leads clinical adoption planning grounded in D5-02 validation evidence; ISIA targets manufacturing licensing via the EIT Manufacturing network; ELI pursues a confirmed logistics integration roadmap for ≥3 international corridors by M48; and CERIA submits ≥1 formal standardisation proposal to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE (D4-01, M48), targeting adoption by ≥3 external multi-agent projects. The consortium adopts an open-core IPR strategy: core MAESTRO framework, benchmark suite, and coordination protocols are released under Apache 2.0; sector-specific demonstrator configurations may be commercially licensed by NIHS, ISIA, and ELI; BAL leads IPR management and IP register maintenance under WP9 (T9-02). Communication activities encompass project website, conference presentations, and targeted outreach to EU AI Act and European AI Strategy policy communities, coordinated through WP9 (D9-01, M6; D9-03, M48). All scientific publications will be made available through open access dissemination in alignment with EU open science practices.

claim_statuses.claim_summary: EO-01: advances in planning, memory, reasoning confirmed via WP2, WP3, WP4 outcomes
EO-02: decentralised multi-agent coordination framework with convergence proof (WP4)
EI-01: Apply AI Strategy — three TRL-5 demonstrators across WP5, WP6, WP7
EI-02: EDIH-mediated industrial uptake across healthcare, manufacturing, ecosystem
EI-03: open scientific advancement via publications and AI-on-demand platform (D8-02, D9-02)
EI-04: EU strategic autonomy pathway — inferred from destination placement only
EI-05: unlocking economic potential via open-source framework and SME ecosystem
OBJ-4 full bundle: 10% accuracy threshold, 500 de-identified cases, 5 diagnostic domains (WP5)
OBJ-6 full bundle: ≥20% recovery improvement, ≥15% adherence improvement, 3 corridors (WP7)
OBJ-7 full bundle: ≥500 GitHub stars, AI-on-demand, ≥2 TEFs, ≥3 EDIHs (WP9, BAL)
